binaries.title: fvevol.sys (BitLocker Drive Encryption) - Patch diff analysis

changed_functions.name: FveEowBuildBootSector (0x1C00033EC unpatched / 0x140012A74 patched)
key_changes: Patched build inserts 'test rdx, rdx; jz loc_140012ADE' at 0x140012AA4-0x140012AA7 on the search-miss fallback path
The inserted test runs AFTER 'movdqu [rdx], xmm0' at 0x140012AA0, which has already dereferenced the same rdx = arg1+0xF0
The GUID-found branch (jnz) targets 0x140012AA9, skipping the added test; it is reachable only on the fallback path
Decompiled patched condition is 'result != 0 || (v7 = v5+240, *(_OWORD*)(v5+240) = EOW_INFORMATION_OFFSET_GUID, v5 != -240)'; the added term is v5 != -240 (i.e. arg1+0xF0 != 0)
changed_functions.security_notes: FveEowBuildBootSector calls FveEowFindGuidRecognition, which scans the caller-supplied buffer (arg1) for the EOW information GUID and returns a pointer to the matching entry or NULL. On a NULL return both builds set v7 = arg1+0xF0, write default GUID data there via 'movdqu [rdx], xmm0' (unpatched 0x1C0003418 / patched 0x140012AA0), and then, if arg3 and arg4 are non-NULL, write through v7 at +0x10, +0x20, and in a two-iteration loop. The only difference is the patched build's inserted 'test rdx, rdx; jz' at 0x140012AA4. This is not a security fix: (1) the test executes after the 'movdqu [rdx], xmm0' at 0x140012AA0 has already dereferenced the same rdx, so it cannot prevent any invalid access; (2) rdx is arg1+0xF0 computed by lea, zero only for the impossible input arg1 == -0xF0 (0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFF10); (3) the sole caller FvepWriteBootSectors passes a driver-allocated pool buffer as arg1, so arg1+0xF0 is a valid in-buffer offset in both builds. The unpatched build is not exposed to a NULL/invalid-pointer dereference on this path, and the patched instruction adds no reachable protection. Code-generation artifact of the OS-build refresh, not a security fix.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
Unpatched FveEowBuildBootSector (0x1C00033EC):
  00000001C00033FD  call    FveEowFindGuidRecognition
  00000001C0003402  mov     rdx, rax
  00000001C0003405  test    rax, rax
  00000001C0003408  jnz     short loc_1C000341C   ; GUID found -> skip fallback
  00000001C000340A  movups  xmm0, cs:EOW_INFORMATION_OFFSET_GUID
  00000001C0003411  lea     rdx, [rcx+0F0h]        ; rdx = arg1 + 0xF0
  00000001C0003418  movdqu  xmmword ptr [rdx], xmm0 ; dereferences rdx
  00000001C000341C  test    rbx, rbx              ; check arg3
  00000001C000341F  jz      short loc_1C0003451
  00000001C0003421  test    r11, r11             ; check arg4
  00000001C0003424  jz      short loc_1C0003451
  00000001C0003433  movups  xmmword ptr [rdx+10h], xmm0
  00000001C0003437  movsd   qword ptr [rdx+20h], xmm1
  00000001C000343C  sub     rdx, r11
  00000001C000343F  mov     rax, [r11]
  00000001C0003442  mov     [rdx+r11+28h], rax

Patched FveEowBuildBootSector (0x140012A74):
  0000000140012A85  call    FveEowFindGuidRecognition
  0000000140012A8A  mov     rdx, rax
  0000000140012A8D  test    rax, rax
  0000000140012A90  jnz     short loc_140012AA9   ; GUID found -> skip fallback AND the added test
  0000000140012A92  movups  xmm0, cs:EOW_INFORMATION_OFFSET_GUID
  0000000140012A99  lea     rdx, [rcx+0F0h]        ; rdx = arg1 + 0xF0
  0000000140012AA0  movdqu  xmmword ptr [rdx], xmm0 ; dereferences rdx (same as unpatched)
  0000000140012AA4  test    rdx, rdx              ; ADDED, but rdx already dereferenced above
  0000000140012AA7  jz      short loc_140012ADE   ; taken only if arg1 == -0xF0 (impossible for a real buffer)
  0000000140012AA9  test    rbx, rbx
  0000000140012AAC  jz      short loc_140012ADE
  0000000140012AAE  test    r11, r11
  0000000140012AB1  jz      short loc_140012ADE
  0000000140012AC0  movups  xmmword ptr [rdx+10h], xmm0
  0000000140012AC4  movsd   qword ptr [rdx+20h], xmm1
  0000000140012AC9  sub     rdx, r11
  0000000140012ACC  mov     rax, [r11]
  0000000140012ACF  mov     [r11+rdx+28h], rax

changed_functions.name: GetKeyRing (0x1C00C2CC8 unpatched / 0x140D8078 patched)
key_changes: Sole caller of MmMapIoSpaceEx in the driver; two call sites (0x1C00C305E and 0x1C00C30B8 unpatched, 0x1400D840D and 0x1400D8467 patched)
The MmMapIoSpaceEx setup (physical address in rcx, size 0x1000 in edx, Protect=4 in r8d) is byte-identical between the two builds at both call sites

The only in-function difference is an equivalent restatement of a NUL/space character test during string parsing (unpatched 'mov ecx,0FFDFh; test [rdx],cx; jz' vs patched 'test ax,ax; jz' plus 'cmp ax,0x20; jz')
changed_functions.security_notes: GetKeyRing parses a decimal ASCII string into a physical address (accumulated in r14 via r14 = r14*10 + digit), maps 0x1000 bytes at that physical address with Protect=4 (PAGE_READWRITE) via MmMapIoSpaceEx, validates the mapped region with FveKeyringIsValid, unmaps it, and remaps using the size read from the mapped header ([rsi+8]). This mapping code is identical in the unpatched and patched builds; this patch does not add any bounds or validation check on the physical address or size. The only change inside the function is a semantically equivalent refactor of a NUL/space character test during string parsing. No security-relevant change was made to this function in this patch.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
First MmMapIoSpaceEx call site is identical across builds.
Unpatched (0x1C00C2CC8):
  00000001C00C304B  mov     ebx, 1000h
  00000001C00C3050  mov     r8d, 4            ; Protect = PAGE_READWRITE
  00000001C00C3056  mov     edx, ebx          ; NumberOfBytes = 0x1000
  00000001C00C3058  mov     rcx, r14          ; PhysicalAddress (parsed from ASCII string)
  00000001C00C305B  mov     r15d, ebx
  00000001C00C305E  call    cs:__imp_MmMapIoSpaceEx
Patched (0x140D8078):
  00000001400D83FA  mov     ebx, 1000h
  00000001400D83FF  mov     r8d, 4
  00000001400D8405  mov     edx, ebx
  00000001400D8407  mov     rcx, r14
  00000001400D840A  mov     r15d, ebx
  00000001400D840D  call    cs:__imp_MmMapIoSpaceEx
Register setup, mapping size, and protection are identical; no validation was added before the mapping call.

changed_functions.name: FveTryInlineWriteEncrypt (0x1C0048B28 unpatched / 0x140079DC0 patched)
key_changes: Size-driven allocation/IoAllocateMdl decision is functionally identical (De-Morgan-refactored); the same length flows to the MDL and to memmove in both builds with no new guard
New code is feature staging (Feature_SteelixInlineNvmeCryptoEngine, Feature_BitLockerEphemeralVolumeSupport) plus a per-CPU AcquireInlineControl/ReleaseInlineControl refactor
changed_functions.security_notes: OS-build churn: allocation size handling is unchanged; new instructions are feature-staging gates and the pool-API modernization. No added bounds or length validation.

changed_functions.name: FveConfigManageLoad (0x1C00222F8 unpatched / 0x14001EEE4 patched)
key_changes: The integer-overflow guard (RtlULongToUShort with a negative-return check) exists identically in both builds
The MmProbeAndLockPages/memmove flow inside KeStackAttachProcess/KeUnstackDetachProcess is byte-for-byte the same in both builds
Differences are the ExAllocatePoolWithTag to ExAllocatePool2 migration and struct-offset shifts
changed_functions.security_notes: The validations present here (integer-overflow guard, probe-and-lock flow) are identical in both builds. No security-relevant change delivered by this patch.

changed_functions.name: FveWipeInfoFileCreate (0x1C008221C unpatched / 0x1400A82B0 patched)
key_changes: Patched build consolidates a manual FveAllocateUnicodePath/FveFileGetPath/ZwCreateFile/ZwFsControlFile(0x9C280) fallback into a second FveFileOpenEx call
Sector-alignment computation is identical between builds
Refactor/consolidation, code removed rather than validation added
changed_functions.security_notes: Structural consolidation of the file-open path. No new bounds or input validation was added.

changed_functions.name: FveInitialDataReadEDrv (0x1C006D52C unpatched / 0x140050F3C patched)
key_changes: Patched build inserts a Feature_BitLockerEphemeralVolumeSupport report and a device/feature-flag gate on internal Context fields (not on attacker-supplied buffer contents)
The pre-existing FveValidateInformation corruption check (FVE_METADATA_CORRUPT_ERROR) is present in both builds
Sensitive-buffer zeroing changed from memset to an inline byte loop over the same buffer/length (codegen); ExAllocatePoolWithTag to ExAllocatePool2 migration
changed_functions.security_notes: The new gate keys off internal device/feature flags, not external input. Existing metadata validation is unchanged. Feature staging plus pool-API modernization, not input validation.

security_findings.entry_point: Called from FvepWriteBootSectors during BitLocker EOW boot-sector handling
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (added pointer test is a no-op guard)
security_findings.description: FveEowBuildBootSector calls FveEowFindGuidRecognition, which scans the caller-supplied buffer (arg1) for the EOW information GUID and returns a pointer to the matching entry or NULL. On a NULL return both builds set v7 = arg1+0xF0 and write default GUID data there via 'movdqu [rdx], xmm0' (unpatched 0x1C0003418 / patched 0x140012AA0), then, if arg3 and arg4 are non-NULL, write through v7 at +0x10, +0x20, and in a two-iteration loop. The only difference between builds is the patched build's inserted 'test rdx, rdx; jz loc_140012ADE' at 0x140012AA4-0x140012AA7. This delivers no security effect: (1) the test runs after 'movdqu [rdx], xmm0' at 0x140012AA0 has already dereferenced the same rdx = arg1+0xF0; (2) rdx is arg1+0xF0 via lea, zero only for the impossible input arg1 == -0xF0; (3) the sole caller FvepWriteBootSectors passes a driver-allocated pool buffer as arg1, so arg1+0xF0 is a valid in-buffer address in both builds. The unpatched build has no reachable NULL/invalid-pointer dereference on this path. Code-generation artifact of the OS-build refresh, not a fix.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: N/A. On the search-miss fallback path both builds write default GUID data to arg1+0xF0 (a valid offset within the driver-allocated buffer supplied by FvepWriteBootSectors) and then the adjacent fields when arg3/arg4 are non-NULL. The patched build's added test is reachable only after the pointer has already been dereferenced and is taken only for the impossible input arg1 == -0xF0, so no observable behavior changes between builds.
security_findings.primitive: None. The added instruction gates no reachable dereference, and the unpatched build is not exposed on this path.
security_findings.assembly:
Unpatched FveEowBuildBootSector (0x1C00033EC):
  00000001C00033FD  call    FveEowFindGuidRecognition
  00000001C0003402  mov     rdx, rax
  00000001C0003405  test    rax, rax
  00000001C0003408  jnz     short loc_1C000341C   ; GUID found -> skip fallback
  00000001C000340A  movups  xmm0, cs:EOW_INFORMATION_OFFSET_GUID
  00000001C0003411  lea     rdx, [rcx+0F0h]        ; rdx = arg1 + 0xF0
  00000001C0003418  movdqu  xmmword ptr [rdx], xmm0 ; dereferences rdx
  00000001C000341C  test    rbx, rbx
  00000001C000341F  jz      short loc_1C0003451
  00000001C0003421  test    r11, r11
  00000001C0003424  jz      short loc_1C0003451
  00000001C0003442  mov     [rdx+r11+28h], rax

Patched FveEowBuildBootSector (0x140012A74):
  0000000140012A85  call    FveEowFindGuidRecognition
  0000000140012A8A  mov     rdx, rax
  0000000140012A8D  test    rax, rax
  0000000140012A90  jnz     short loc_140012AA9   ; GUID found -> skips fallback AND the added test
  0000000140012A92  movups  xmm0, cs:EOW_INFORMATION_OFFSET_GUID
  0000000140012A99  lea     rdx, [rcx+0F0h]        ; rdx = arg1 + 0xF0
  0000000140012AA0  movdqu  xmmword ptr [rdx], xmm0 ; dereferences rdx (same as unpatched)
  0000000140012AA4  test    rdx, rdx              ; ADDED, but rdx already dereferenced above
  0000000140012AA7  jz      short loc_140012ADE   ; taken only if arg1 == -0xF0
  0000000140012AA9  test    rbx, rbx
  0000000140012ACF  mov     [r11+rdx+28h], rax

breakpoints.why: Break at entry to observe arg1 (rcx, the driver-allocated buffer), arg3 (r8), arg4 (r9).
After the search call. rax=0 selects the fallback path; rdx becomes rcx+0xF0 at 0x1C0003411.
The fallback write 'movdqu [rdx], xmm0' dereferences rdx = arg1+0xF0. In the patched build the added 'test rdx, rdx' at 0x140012AA4 runs after this same write, so it cannot prevent an access.

watch: rax at 0x1C0003402 = return of FveEowFindGuidRecognition (0 selects the fallback path)
rdx at 0x1C0003411 = arg1+0xF0 (an offset inside the caller's driver-allocated buffer)
rbx (r8) = arg3 - must be non-NULL to reach the write block
r11 (r9) = arg4 - must be non-NULL to reach the write block
rcx = arg1 (driver-allocated pool buffer supplied by FvepWriteBootSectors)
key_offsets: 0x1C00033FD: call FveEowFindGuidRecognition - buffer search that can return NULL
0x1C0003418 / patched 0x140012AA0: movdqu [rdx], xmm0 - fallback write that dereferences rdx
Patched 0x140012AA4: test rdx, rdx - the added instruction, executed after the dereference above
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: N/A - no vulnerability to trigger. On the fallback path both builds write to arg1+0xF0, a valid offset inside the caller's driver-allocated buffer.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On both binaries the fallback path writes default GUID data to arg1+0xF0 and, when arg3/arg4 are non-NULL, the adjacent fields. In the patched build 'test rdx, rdx' at 0x140012AA4 finds rdx non-zero for any real buffer, so the branch at 0x140012AA7 is not taken.
debugging_aids.notes: The caller FvepWriteBootSectors passes a driver-allocated pool buffer as arg1. FveEowFindGuidRecognition is a buffer search, not an allocator; its NULL return means the EOW GUID was not found, in which case the code uses the default in-buffer offset arg1+0xF0.

security_findings.entry_point: Physical memory mapping via MmMapIoSpaceEx - sole caller of MmMapIoSpaceEx in the driver
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No patch-introduced change (physical-memory mapping path identical in both builds)
security_findings.description: GetKeyRing is the only function in fvevol.sys that calls MmMapIoSpaceEx. It parses a decimal ASCII string into a physical address (accumulated in r14 via r14 = r14*10 + digit), maps 0x1000 bytes at that physical address with Protect=4 (PAGE_READWRITE) at the first call site (0x1C00C305E), validates the mapped region with FveKeyringIsValid, unmaps it via MmUnmapIoSpace, and remaps using the size read from the mapped header ([rsi+8]) at the second call site (0x1C00C30B8). The MmMapIoSpaceEx register setup - physical address in rcx, NumberOfBytes in edx, Protect in r8d - is byte-identical between the unpatched and patched builds at both call sites. This patch makes no change to the mapping path. The only in-function difference is a semantically equivalent restatement of a NUL/space character test during string parsing.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: N/A for this patch. The physical-memory mapping behavior of GetKeyRing is identical in the unpatched and patched drivers; no security condition was introduced or removed by this patch.
security_findings.primitive: None introduced by this patch. The mapping of a string-parsed physical address (0x1000 bytes, PAGE_READWRITE) is present identically in both builds and is not modified here.
security_findings.evidence:
First MmMapIoSpaceEx call site, identical across builds.
Unpatched:
  00000001C00C304B  mov ebx, 1000h
  00000001C00C3050  mov r8d, 4            ; Protect = PAGE_READWRITE
  00000001C00C3056  mov edx, ebx          ; NumberOfBytes = 0x1000
  00000001C00C3058  mov rcx, r14          ; PhysicalAddress (parsed from ASCII string)
  00000001C00C305E  call cs:__imp_MmMapIoSpaceEx
Patched:
  00000001400D83FA  mov ebx, 1000h
  00000001400D83FF  mov r8d, 4
  00000001400D8405  mov edx, ebx
  00000001400D8407  mov rcx, r14
  00000001400D840D  call cs:__imp_MmMapIoSpaceEx
The only difference inside GetKeyRing is a NUL/space character test refactor during string parsing (unpatched 'mov ecx,0FFDFh; test [rdx],cx; jz' vs patched 'test ax,ax; jz' plus 'cmp ax,0x20; jz'), which is semantically equivalent.
security_findings.assembly: Both call sites (0x1C00C305E and 0x1C00C30B8 unpatched; 0x1400D840D and 0x1400D8467 patched) set up MmMapIoSpaceEx identically across builds: PhysicalAddress in rcx (from r14), NumberOfBytes in edx (0x1000 at the first site, [rsi+8] at the second), Protect=4 in r8d. No conditional check or comparison was added before the mapping call in the patched build.

breakpoints.why: Break at entry to observe how the physical address is parsed (accumulated in r14) and how the mapping parameters are prepared for MmMapIoSpaceEx.
First MmMapIoSpaceEx call site. rcx holds the physical address (from r14), edx holds NumberOfBytes (0x1000), r8d holds Protect (4). The same setup appears in the patched build at 0x1400D840D.
Second MmMapIoSpaceEx call site, remapping with the size read from the mapped header ([rsi+8]). Identical setup in the patched build at 0x1400D8467.

watch: rcx = PhysicalAddress parsed from the ASCII string (accumulated in r14)
edx = NumberOfBytes (0x1000 at the first call, [rsi+8] at the second)
Comparing the instructions around 0x1C00C305E with the patched build at 0x1400D840D shows identical setup - no validation was added
key_offsets: The MmMapIoSpaceEx register setup is byte-identical across builds; the only in-function change is a NUL/space character-test refactor during string parsing
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: N/A for this patch - the mapping behavior is unchanged between builds.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On both binaries: MmMapIoSpaceEx is called with the string-parsed physical address in rcx, 0x1000 (then [rsi+8]) in edx, and Protect=4 in r8d. There is no added validation in the patched build.
debugging_aids.notes: The two MmMapIoSpaceEx call sites serve the map/validate/unmap/remap flow (first a fixed 0x1000-byte probe, then a remap using the header-reported size). Both are identical across builds; this patch does not modify the physical-memory mapping path.

summary: The patch diff of fvevol.sys (BitLocker Drive Encryption driver) spans 1140 changed functions across a broad OS-version difference (0.7398 similarity). No security-relevant change was delivered. The one instruction-level edit that survived scrutiny is in FveEowBuildBootSector: the patched build inserts 'test rdx, rdx; jz' at 0x140012AA4 on the search-miss fallback path, but that test runs after the preceding 'movdqu [rdx], xmm0' at 0x140012AA0 has already dereferenced the same rdx = arg1+0xF0, and rdx is zero only for the impossible input arg1 == -0xF0; the sole caller FvepWriteBootSectors passes a driver-allocated pool buffer as arg1, so the fallback pointer is a valid in-buffer offset in both builds. The instruction prevents no reachable dereference and is a code-generation artifact, not a fix. GetKeyRing (sole MmMapIoSpaceEx caller) has a byte-identical mapping path in both builds; its only in-function change is a semantically equivalent NUL/space character-test refactor. The low-similarity behavioral functions (FveTryInlineWriteEncrypt, FveConfigManageLoad, FveWipeInfoFileCreate, FveInitialDataReadEDrv) differ only by ExAllocatePool2 migration, feature-staging gates, WPP/ETW tracing, and register-allocation/struct-offset churn; their existing validations are present identically in both builds.
